title: Provincial Road Management Facility (PRMF)
long_description: The Provincial Road Management Facility (PRMF) assists the Philippine Government rehabilitate and maintain more than 600km of provincial roads in up to 10 provinces in Mindanao and the Visayas. PRMF aims to boost the local economy by improving road transport so that more people will have better access to rehabilitated and maintained road networks, economic activity and public infrastructure and services that will improve their livelihoods. Initial reports document improvements for communities in: travel times (a reduction by as much as 50 per cent in some areas), employment opportunities for local unskilled workers, economic activity for businesses along roads, increased investment by farmers in agricultural production.  The total value of this initiative is $81 million over 6 years, starting 2009-10.

title: Public Financial Management Program for Institutions and Infrastructure (PFMP II)
short_description: PUBLIC FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT PROGRAM FOR INSTITUTIONS AND INFRASTRUCTURE (PFMP II)
long_description: Public Financial Management Program for Institutions and Infrastructure (PFMP II)

title: Samoa Economic Infrastructure
long_description: The Samoa Economic Infrastructure Program aims to stimulate sustainable economic activity in Samoa and provide improved access to services and markets by investing in economic infrastructure. The program comprises three components: investments in priority economic infrastructure projects, infrastructure structural and policy reforms performance linked aid program, and a technical assistance fund. The program uses established and experienced infrastructure partners such as the World Bank and the ADB to implement specific infrastructure investments. The total value of this initiative is $20.0 million over 4 years, starting 2013/2014.

title: Eastern Indonesia National Roads Improvement Project

long_description: Eastern Indonesia National Roads Improvement Project

title: Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program Phase 2
short_description: VANUATU TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM PHASE 2
long_description: The Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program (VTSSP) will be a long term, 10 to 15 year sector based program delivered through a number of phases. The first phase was a 5 year program working with the Vanuatu Public Works Department to complete three sub-projects and related works on the islands of Tanna, Malekula and Ambae, and other works on these three islands. Phase 1 of VTSSP rehabilitated 10% of the rural road network, generating employment including for women. Funding of this initiative is for VTSSP Phase 2 which aims to: ensure the Ministry of Infrastructure and Public Utilities (MIPU) and the Public Works Department (PWD) has the skills, systems and resources necessary to plan and manage its core national road management mandate,  and that the PWD effectively maintains key road transport infrastructure in all provinces. The total value of this initiative is $27.3 million over 4 years, starting 2012-13.

title: Roads for Development - R4D
long_description: The Roads for Development program scales up the effective work undertaken by the Investment Budget Execution Support for Rural Infrastructure Development and Employment Generation (TIM Works) project. Its objective is to establish and institutionalise a rural road system for effectively planning, budgeting and managing rural road works using labour based methods, as appropriate. The program will enable more rural women and men to benefit from improved access to roads and therefore access to markets. Improved roads will help farmers to earn higher and more sustained income and will make it easier for people to access health centres, schools and markets. This program will rehabilitate and maintain rural roads and will ensure that at least 1,150km, more than a third of the rural road network in Timor-Leste is kept in good condition. Where possible labour intensive approaches will be used that will maximise the employment opportunities for people in rural areas. This could result in short-term employment for over 50,000 people, providing much needed work experience and extra cash to village communities. The total value of this initiative is $30.5 million over five years, starting 2011-12.

title: Kiribati Infrastructure
long_description: Poor infrastructure is a key inhibitor to economic growth in Kiribati. In recognising the importance of improved economic infrastructure provision, AusAID and the Government of Kiribati (GoK) agreed, during partnership talks held in September 2012, to add infrastructure as the fourth priority under the Kiribati-Australia Partnership for Development. This initiative provides support for economic infrastructure in Kiribati in cooperation with other development partners through the Pacific Region Infrastructure Facility (PRIF). Through PRIF, AusAID funds other development partners – such as the World Bank and Asian Development Bank (ADB) – to deliver infrastructure projects in Kiribati. Support is provided in sub sectors of: transport, road rehabilitation, water sanitation, energy and ICT. The total value of this initiative is $34.4 million over five years, starting 2011-12.

title: Indonesia Infrastructure Initiative
long_description: The Australian Government launched the Infrastructure for Growth Initiative (IFGI) in 2007 to help regional partners address their pressing infrastructure needs. IFGI is directed towards helping regional partner government improve their infrastructure policies and finance high-priority infrastructure in conjunction with other international donors. The Indonesia Infrastructure Initiative (IndII) has been developed within the IFGI framework and is supported by funding under IFGI. and aims to support national and sub-national governments' efforts to address constraints on infrastructure investment and enable efficient and effective infrastructure service delivery. The total value of this initiative is $178.6 million over 8 years, starting 2007-08.

title: Cambodia Infrastructure Investment 2013-2020
long_description: Funding under this initiative provides support for infrastructure investments in Cambodia. While Cambodia has experienced high rates of economic growth over the past twenty years, continued development gains are hampered by low levels of infrastructure development particularly in rural areas. Activities under this initiative will be cofinanced infrastructure activities that align with Australia's broader strategic interests in Cambodia, particularly economic growth.  The initial investment is $7.6million, for an activity that will be implemented until 2020, with other activities possible over time.

title: Solomon Islands Transport Sector Based Approach
short_description: SOLOMON ISLANDS TRANSPORT SECTOR BASED APPROACH
long_description: The Solomon Islands Transport Sector Based Approach initiative will support the Solomon Islands Government implement a series of activities in the Transport sector including the Transport Sector Development Project, Malaita Road works, Technical Assistance support to the Solomon Islands Government Ministry of Infrastructure Development and design and Fiduciary risk assessment activities. Majority of funding to the Sector will be channelled through the National Transport Fund to support the priorities under the National Transport Plan. The total value of this initiative is $37.74 million over 5 years, starting 2010-11.

title: Indonesia Transport Safety Assistance Package
long_description: The Indonesia Transport Safety Assistance Package (ITSAP) is designed to contribute to improved transport safety in Indonesia. ITSAP will assist Indonesia to regulate and promote transport safety in accordance with applicable international standards and contemporary safety management practices, consistent with the priorities of the Indonesian Government, by providing technical assistance, capacity building, and advise on improved governance and safety management practices and procedures. The first phase of ITSAP began in 2007 following the Garuda air crash in Yogyakarta. Since 2007, the Department of Infrastructure and Regional Development has administered the $38.4 million ITSAP program, which is funded by Australia's Official Development Assistance. Assistance is delivered by Australian agencies including Air services Australia, the Australian Maritime Safety Authority, the Australian Transport Safety Bureau and the Civil Aviation Safety Authority to their Indonesian counterparts including the Directorate General of Civil Aviation, the Directorate General of Sea Transportation and the Directorate General of Land Transportation within the Ministry of Transportation.  Australian agencies also work with the National Transportation Safety Committee, the National Search and Rescue Agency and AirNav Indonesia. The total value of this initiative is $4.6 million expensed during the 2014-15 financial year.

long_description: TransJakarta, the first and longest Bus rapid transit (BRT) system in Asia. The Institute for Transportation and Development Policy (ITDP) has been asked to help physically integrate it with the new Metro, optimize its services, improve NMT access, and help the city reform new zoning and parking regulations in the city. A goal is to upgrade TransJakarta to Gold Standard BRT and change zoning and parking regulations to be consistent with Gold Standard transit oriented development.ITDP has also been asked by the Mayor of Medan to develop a conceptual plan for the first full BRT outside Jakarta.

agency: Federal Ministry for the Enviroment, Climate Action, Nature Conservation and Nuclear Safety
title: Sino-German cooperation on low carbon transport
short_description: SINO-GERMAN COOPERATION ON LOW CARBON TRANSPORT
long_description: The objective of this project is to ensure that decision-makers in relevant government departments have strategies at their disposal that enable them to develop China's transport sector in a climate-friendly manner. The project advises the Chinese Government via several channels focusing on the development of a monitoring, reporting, verification (MRV) system for the transport sector. The implementation of pilot projects will lead to direct reductions in emissions, while the development of mitigation capacity will lead to indirect reductions.

title: India Sustainable Mobility Initiative
long_description: The Institute for Transportation and Development Policy (ITDP)'s India Sustainable Mobility Initiative will focus on the development of effective state-level urban transport policies and capacity building in the states of Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu. The aim is that by the end of the grant, state policies will promote high quality BRT together with zoning and parking reform, to dramatically cut greenhouse gas emissions.

agency: Federal Ministry for the Enviroment, Climate Action, Nature Conservation and Nuclear Safety
title: Transitioning to Low Carbon Sea Transport
short_description: TRANSITIONING TO LOW CARBON SEA TRANSPORT
long_description: The Republic of Marshall Islands' (RMI) NDCs include emission reductions from transport. Fossil fuels for transport are a major part of RMI's import balance. The project will contribute to reduction of greenhouse gas emissions from shipping by supporting RMI to meet its NDC targets. In response to RMI's framework for transition to low carbon transport, and with the Micronesian Sustainable Transport Centre, emissions and economics of the domestic fleet is assessed, low carbon technologies for intra and inter-atoll shipping trialed, and education and training provided. Policy support to Government is provided throughout two phases, including strengthening the High Ambition Coalition for the negotiations at UNFCCC, the International Maritime Organization (IMO) and other committees. By testing innovative technology the project will stimulate further action towards emission reduction in low-carbon sea transport, within RMI, the Micronesian region, and other Small Island Developing States.

Energising Mobility and Transport in China - Mobility+ Fuels Strategy as a Contribution to the Traffic Transition in China - Pilot Jing-Jin-Ji Region
short_description: ENERGISING MOBILITY AND TRANSPORT IN CHINA - MOBILITY+ FUELS STRATEGY AS A CONTRIBUTION TO THE TRAFFIC TRANSITION IN CHINA - PILOT JING-JIN-JI REGION
long_description: The project is exploring various options for a mobility and fuels strategy (MFS) in China. How can policy-makers drive the process towards future-proof mobility? What propulsion technologies, fuels, measures and regulations are already available and future-proof? These are all important questions that feature strongly in the issues addressed by the German Government's MFS. Implemented by the Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH on behalf of Germany's Federal Ministry of Transport and Digital Infrastructure (BMVI), the MFS pilot project in China got off the ground in January 2017. It follows on from a project in which GIZ cooperated with the Chinese Transport Ministry and its think-tank TPRI (Transport Planning and Research Institute) to identify the latest development trends, barriers and prospects surrounding key segments of an MFS in China.

agency: Swiss Agency for Development and Co-operation
title: Motorable Local Roads Bridge Programme (MLRBP)
long_description: Lack of access aggravated by absence of bridges and adequately maintained local roads is one of the primary reasons for high incidence of poverty in Nepal. The increased confidence of the Government of Nepal in the Swiss Technical Assistance has led the Government to double its budget in this sector, bearing also in mind the need to ensure easy access for reconstruction to the earthquakes affected areas. As part of Switzerland's contribution to reconstruction, SDC will support the preparation of 100 additional bridges until the end of 2015.

agency: Swiss Agency for Development and Co-operation
title: Thin lines that change lives
long_description: In Nepal, trail bridges are vital for rural mobility. Almost 80 of them were severely damaged or destroyed during the earthquakes of 2015, jeopardizing access to education, health services and local markets for thousands of inhabitants in the mountainous regions. The SDC joined the reconstruction efforts, basing its intervention on the long-standing expertise it has in the country.

short_description: DECENTRALIZED RURAL INFRASTRUCTURE AND LIVELIHOOD PROJECT (DRILP)
long_description: DRILP Phase 3 will support the Government of Nepal to accelerate recovery and reconstruction following the devastating earthquake of 25 April 2015 and the major aftershock of 12 May 2015. The project will provide the Technical Assistance (TA) to implement the Asian Development Bank (ADB) financed Earthquake Emergency Assistance Project in rebuilding 450 kms of damaged roads in 12 districts[1] of Nepal and rehabilitate 200 kms of trails in two[2] districts. [1] The 12 earthquake affected project districts are Solukhumbu, Okhaldhunga, Ramechhap, Dolakha, Kavre, Sindhuli, Kathmandu, Lalitpur, Bhaktapur, Lamjung, Gorkha and Chitwan [2] Dolakha and Gorkha

agency: Swiss Agency for Development and Co-operation
title: Motorable Local Roads Bridge Programme (MLRBP)
long_description: Lack of access to services, markets and opportunities is one of the reasons for high incidence of poverty in Nepal. The Government of Nepal (GoN) has accorded a high priority to the construction of bridges to improve accessibility of remote areas in Nepal and increased the budget 5-fold in the last four years. The proposed phase will build on GoN's confidence in the Swiss technical support, while preparing to accelerate the construction of bridges to facilitate improved access.

agency: Swiss Agency for Development and Co-operation
title: Local Roads Improvement Programme (LRIP)
long_description: Past SDC interventions have focussed on making rural road construction work for the poor and disadvantaged through labour-intensive construction methods. This project now takes proven approaches further by ensuring even more all-weather accessibility to markets and services by emphasising the importance of maintenance and preservation of built roads through promotion of labour-based roads maintenance methods and by fostering an institutional process in the districts, which should pave the way for the highly successful concepts of road construction and maintenance to be adopted as national standards.

title: Khujand public transport project
long_description: The SECO-EBRD project aims to improve passenger transport services in Greater Khujand. The project entails investments, regulatory support, Corporate Development support for the public transport company and a stakeholder engagement program. The metropolitan area of Greater Khujand is experiencing rapid urbanisation and population growth, which is aggravated through a high number of returning economic migrants from Russia. The issue of public transport is key to economic inclusion and a major challenge for Khujand's urban development. Women and the poorer part of the population tend to have less access to individual transport by car and are estimated to benefit disproportionately from the access to improved public transport services.

agency: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills
title: Cities and Infrastructure Programme 2017
long_description: This research explores how low-income communities, private energy entrepreneurs, and government (at various scales) work in contestation and collaboration to devise and deliver affordable domestic energy that meets the long-term needs and aspirations of low-income households in two rapidly urbanising cities, Bangalore (India) and Cape Town (South Africa). The primary focus is on the role of the three key stakeholders, investigating how government and industry plan and implement energy innovation in government subsidised housing, and the role of low-income households' needs and aspirations in this process. The research has two overarching aims: firstly, to implement an interdisciplinary approach that delivers learning across the physical and social sciences, and secondly, to propose strategies that enable divergent stakeholders and institutions to work collaboratively in producing and implementing innovative energy solutions that are technically-, financially- and culturally-appropriate for government-subsidised housing (in terms of both people and product).

agency: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills
title: Cities and Infrastructure Programme 2017
long_description: Continuous and widespread Violence Against Women (VAW) in urban India highlight the challenge of delivering SDGs 5 and 11 - gender equality and safe, sustainable, inclusive cities. In particular, women in low-income urban neighbourhoods face increased sexual and physical assaults during access to and use of connected infrastructures (eg. water, toilets, transport, walkways), which also highlight the challenge of delivering SDG 6 – clean water and sanitation to all. Combined with this is an acute information and skills gap in technology use amongst these women that impedes their knowledgeable and empowered engagement with social and material assemblages of urban infrastructures. This project will take a rights-based approach to the challenge: How to address VAW by improving women's knowledge of and safe access to urban infrastructure in the Indian city. The project will use innovations in digital technology and open source mapping, co-produced with societal partners, to collect big data on infrastructural blindspots, and deep data on VAW through participatory mapping of infrastructure use.

agency: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills
title: Cities and Infrastructure Programme 2017
long_description: The project is a multi-disciplinary study of the consequences of dislocation for poor communities in Lahore (Pakistan) and Colombo (Sri Lanka). In both cases, families living in low-income settlements have been forcibly relocated in the name of development. This study will focus on the underexplored implications of relocation for everyday relations of kinship and care, which are vitally important for the resilience of poor communities in Asia. Anthropological research on networks of kinship will be complemented by planning expertise to produce maps of displacement. The project is based on many years' experience of research in the two cities and the team will draw on a wide pool of collaborators at different points in the research. In particular, the team will use evidence in engagements with local activists, art practitioners, lawyers, architects and planners, and policymakers. Within both settings, the project will develop new approaches to the built environment that minimise the negative effects of dominant infrastructure and displacement interventions on community resilience.

agency: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills
title: Cities and Infrastructure Programme 2017
long_description: Most urban families in developing countries recover from disasters using their own resources, knowledge and initiative ('self-recovery'). Yet, self-recovery is poorly understood, there is a lack of evidence on how to best support it, particularly in urban environments. This project examines self-recovery processes with a focus on the safety and durability of self-built homes after rapid-onset disasters in marginal and informal urban settlements. Findings will inform design of guidelines and tools for urban governments, policy makers and the international humanitarian shelter sector to better promote and support self-recovery, leading to more resilient recovery and reconstruction. There is almost no research on self-recovery from the perspective of affected families. This project addresses this knowledge gap and develops a model for supporting affected people to make informed decisions about their own recovery and future resilience.

title: Developing transport connectivity in China's City Cluster in the Middle Reaches of the Yangtze River
short_description: DEVELOPING TRANSPORT CONNECTIVITY IN CHINA'S CITY CLUSTER IN THE MIDDLE REACHES OF THE YANGTZE RIVER
long_description: The project is designed to use policies and best practice from the UK's Northern Powerhouse to develop transport connectivity in China's City Cluster in the Middle Reaches of the Yangtze River. By June 2017, it is intended that the National Development and Reform Commission, Ministry of Transport and Wuhan government will adopt integrated physical and digitally connected transport planning polices and measures, that reflect UK expertise. This is also expected to generate opportunities for international businesses, including UK ones.

title: Supporting the development of the New Energy Vehicle industry in China
short_description: SUPPORTING THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE NEW ENERGY VEHICLE INDUSTRY IN CHINA
long_description: The project is designed to share UK experience to support the development of the New Energy Vehicle Industry in China: firstly, on policies and incentives for charging facilities, secondly, on innovation and technology in vehicles. It is intended that recommendations will be put forward to the Chinese authorities proposing detailed planning of charging facilities and adoption of a technology roadmap. If taken up by China, international businesses, including UK ones, will be in a better position to understand Chinese policies and identify trade and investment opportunities.

agency: Department for Business, Innovation and Skills
title: Cities and Infrastructure Programme 2017
long_description: Kathmandu's medieval cities and shrines are exceptional architectural and artistic achievements with traditions of seismic adaptation. They host urban infrastructure of tangible and intangible value and play vital roles of cohesion in the life of thousands and represent portals where the heavens touch earth and people commune with guiding deities. Generating 7.6% GDP through tourism, Kathmandu's iconic skyline was dramatically altered by the Gorkha Earthquake in 2015. Losses included 9,000 killed, 20,000 injured and the destruction of 500,000 homes. A total of 691 historic monuments across Nepal were damaged, of which 131 collapsed causing death and injury, with an associated 32% cancellation of tourist visits. This interdisciplinary North-South partnership contributes to SDG17 by co-producing and disseminating a methodology to assess, evaluate and improve the seismic safety of historic urban infrastructure within Kathmandu's World Heritage sites, reducing direct risk to life and livelihoods and damage of gains towards SDG11, while preserving Kathmandu's authenticity and traditions.

title: Access to the right to a decent habitabilit and safe water in Talupula district of Anantapur, India
short_description: ACCESS TO THE RIGHT TO A DECENT HABITABILIT AND SAFE WATER IN TALUPULA DISTRICT OF ANANTAPUR, INDIA
long_description: El enfoque de la habitabilidad básica permite que el proyecto integre el derecho a una vivienda digna, el derecho al agua y saneamiento, y la disponibilidad de servicios e infraestructuras como electricidad y vías urbanas. Es el resultado del trabajo entre los beneficiarios/as, el Comité de Desarrollo Comunitario (CDC) y la contraparte local, con la colaboración del gobierno local y los vecinos y vecinas. Se construirán 83 viviendas dignas en las aldeas de Mulapalli y Ganjivaripalli, asegurando jurídicamente la propiedad al 96,4% de las mujeres. Desde una perspectiva estratégica, se va a incidir en el suministro de agua segura en las dos aldeas. En una de ellas se ha optado por el suministro en todas las viviendas de la aldea, y la otra con acceso a través de grifos públicos suficientes. Esta nueva experiencia para la entidad, avalada en la identificación con un análisis del recurso hídrico, va a servir para estudiar las dos opciones. Es importante también dotar a los asentamientos de un entorno habitable: se construirán baños con letrinas individuales, se llevará el tendido eléctrico, y se mejorará el urbanismo de la comunidad con un trazado homogéneo y asfaltado de las calles. Sus asentamientos actuales no satisfacen ninguno de estos derechos y necesidades básicas. Habitan en chozas deterioradas, poco salubres e inseguras, que perpetúan su vulnerabilidad, especialmente de las mujeres. Las circunstancias de cada comunidad difieren en los detalles, pero todos se encuentran por debajo del umbral de la pobreza, pertenecen o bien a castas bajas o son dálits, con una historia de exclusión a sus espaldas determinada por nacimiento. Un total de 321 personas, 46,73% mujeres y niñas, tendrán acceso a habitabilidad básica completa con vivienda, agua, electricidad y saneamiento, y en adicción 355 personas, el 47,88% mujeres y niñas, tendrán acceso a agua segura. Se potenciará la capacidad de autogestión de la comunidad, potenciando la participación de las mujeres, tanto en el origen, ejecución y seguimiento de las obras, consolidando sus aptitudes y confianza en sí mismas, como personas y como colectivo.

title: Accelerating Sustainable Public-Private Investment for Infrastructure Renewal (ASPIRE)/Accroissement des investissements public privé durables pour le renouvellement des infrastructures
short_description: ACCELERATING SUSTAINABLE PUBLIC-PRIVATE INVESTMENT FOR INFRASTRUCTURE RENEWAL (ASPIRE)/ACCROISSEMENT DES INVESTISSEMENTS PUBLIC PRIVÉ DURABLES POUR LE
long_description: This initiative aims to facilitate the preparation and structuring of bankable Public Private Partnership (PPP) projects to increase investment in infrastructure to support sustainable economic growth in Indonesia. The project includes two interrelated components. The first aims to establish and operationalize a PPP Centre to act as the country's lead agency in PPP project development. The second provides critical complementary support to develop a strong PPP project pipeline, lay the legal and regulatory framework for PPPs in infrastructure, and catalyze early bankable PPP projects. These early investments are intended to demonstrate the value of the PPPs in addressing sustainable infrastructure for poverty reduction and increased access to markets, with an emphasis on the transportation sector, an area of strong Canadian expertise. The project also allows the Indonesia PPP Centre to receive support from the APEC PPP Expert Advisory Panel and to guide the development of PPPs projects in Asia./ Cette initiative vise à faciliter la préparation et le montage de projets de partenariats publics-privés (PPP) susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier pour accroître l'investissement dans l'infrastructure pour appuyer la croissance économique durable en Indonésie. Le projet comprend deux composantes interreliées. La première vise à établir un centre des PPP et à le mettre en service pour agir à titre d'agence principale du pays dans l'élaboration de projets de PPP. La deuxième fournit un soutien complémentaire essentiel pour développer une solide filière de projets de PPP, élaborer le cadre juridique et réglementaire pour les PPP dans l'infrastructure, et jouer un rôle de catalyseur dans les premiers projets de PPP susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier. Ces premiers investissements sont destinés à démontrer la valeur des PPP lorsqu'il s'agit de prendre des mesures à l'égard de l'infrastructure durable pour la réduction de la pauvreté et un accès accru aux marchés, en mettant l'accent sur le secteur des transports, un domaine où le Canada possède une grande expertise. Le projet permet également au Centre de PPP de l'Indonésie de recevoir du soutien du Comité consultatif d'experts de l'APEC sur les PPP et de guider l'élaboration de projets de PPP en Asie.

title: Private Sector Engagement for Agricultural Development/Participation du secteur privé pour le développement agricole
short_description: PRIVATE SECTOR ENGAGEMENT FOR AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT/PARTICIPATION DU SECTEUR PRIVÉ POUR LE DÉVELOPPEMENT AGRICOLE
long_description: The project aims to improve sustainable rural growth for 7,500 farmers in the Mekong delta and Central Highlands of Vietnam. The project contributes to the Government of Vietnam's reform efforts in sustainable economic growth and works to increase competitiveness in the agriculture sector by engaging the private sector.The project focuses on: (i) increasing lending for agricultural activities by private sector banks to give farmers the means to invest in the most profitable seeds, techniques and storage equipment, and to give them the flexibility to wait for the ideal time to sell their goods at better prices, (ii) providing training to farmers to help them improve their agricultural practices, by diversifying their income streams, and improving their financial management, for example, and (iii) improving the handling and storage of crops by farmers, agribusinesses and warehouses to ensure the quality, quantity, and market value of the crops is maintained. The project makes a particular effort to ensure that women farmers have equitable access to financial services and training.Some project activities include: (i) advising up to five private sector banks already engaged in agricultural lending to help them increase their lending, develop appropriate financial products for selected markets, including tailored products for women farmers, (ii) providing training of trainers to 300 people (40 percent women) on how to change farming practices to mitigate risks such as climate, pests, price shock, and marketing, and (iii) supporting agribusinesses to undertake risk analysis of post-harvest losses in order to design tailored solutions for farmers, and training 200 trainers to deliver training on these solutions to farmer groups./ Le projet profitera à 7 500 agriculteurs du delta du Mékong et des hautes terres centrales du Vietnam. Ce projet vise à contribuer aux efforts de réforme réalisés par le gouvernement du Vietnam en matière de croissance économique durable et vise à accroître la compétitivité dans le secteur de l'agriculture par la participation du secteur privé.Le projet met l'accent sur : i) l'augmentation du nombre de prêts octroyés au secteur agricole par les banques du secteur privé afin de donner aux agriculteurs les moyens d'investir dans les semences, les techniques et l'équipement de stockage les plus rentables, et de leur donner la possibilité d'attendre le moment idéal pour vendre leurs produits à meilleur prix, ii) la formation des agriculteurs en matière de pratiques agricoles améliorées, en diversifiant leurs sources de revenus et en améliorant leur gestion financière, par exemple, et iii) l'amélioration de la manutention et de l'entreposage des récoltes des agriculteurs, des entreprises agricoles et des entrepôts afin d'assurer le maintien de la qualité, quantité et valeur de marché des cultures. Le projet fait un effort particulier pour assurer que les femmes agriculteurs aient un accès équitable aux services financiers et à la f

title: Safe Food for Growth/Salubrité alimentaire pour la croissance
short_description: SAFE FOOD FOR GROWTH/SALUBRITÉ ALIMENTAIRE POUR LA CROISSANCE
long_description: This project was announced by MINA in November 2016 at the APEC Leaders' Summit in Peru.The project aims to improve food safety regulation and enforcement in Vietnam, increase competitiveness of poor farmers and other actors supplying safe agri-food products, and increase consumer demand for safe and affordable agri-food. The key beneficiaries include key government ministries at national and sub-national levels, the private sector, and Vietnamese smallholder farmers and consumers, both locally and internationally.The project operates at all stages of the agri-food value chains by engaging three main target groups: (1) the public sector (enabling environment), (2) the private sector (supply side), and (3) the consumer (demand side).Project activities include: (1) the public sector: technical assistance to support institutional reforms and improve policy and operational coordination of government food safety responsibilities at the national and sub-national levels, training and equipment to improve food testing capacity in support of the food safety inspection system, and training and equipment to apply a risk-based approach to food safety, (2) the private sector: gender-responsive technical assistance on good agricultural practices, proper use of chemicals, food handling, packaging and labeling, exporter training in international food safety and quality standards, technical assistance, training and materials for improved traceability of agri-food products, and facilitation of increased application of innovative approaches, technologies, and practices amongst entrepreneurs, and (3) the consumer: consumer education with a focus on civil society including women's organizations to demand safe food, media engagement on food safety issues, and support for the publication of annual State of Food Safety reports./ Ce projet vise à améliorer la réglementation sur la salubrité alimentaire et son application au Vietnam, à augmenter la compétitivité des fermiers les plus pauvres et de fournisseurs de produits agroalimentaires sains et à accroître la demande des consommateurs pour des produits salubres à des prix abordables. Les principaux bénéficiaires comprennent : des ministères clés à l'échelle nationale et infranationale, le secteur privé et les petits exploitants agricoles, ainsi que les consommateurs au Vietnam et à l'étranger.Le projet s'applique à toutes les étapes de la chaîne de valeur agroalimentaire en mobilisant trois principaux groupes cibles : 1) le secteur public (créateur d'un environnement favorable), 2) le secteur privé (volet de l'offre) et 3) le consommateur (volet de la demande).Les activités du projet comprennent : 1) secteur public : de l'assistance technique pour appuyer les réformes institutionnelles et améliorer la coordination stratégique et opérationnelle des responsabilités gouvernementales en matière de salubrité alimentaire à l'échelle nationale et infranationale, de la formation et de l'équipement afin d'améliorer les capacit
purpose: Education and training in transport and storage

title: Technical Assistance - Airport Geographic Information System Technical Assistance, through Hi-Tec Systems, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIRPORT GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION SYSTEM TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH HI-TEC SYSTEMS, INC.
long_description: This grant supported technical assistance (TA) to the Airports Authority of India (AAI) to develop and test a prototype Airport Geographic Information System (AGIS). AAI and Hi-Tec Systems, Inc. jointly proposed this sole-source project to USTDA as a priority for the U.S.-India Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). AGIS can store aeronautical geographic information in an electronic format, which can be updated, edited, and shared within AAI as well as with other airports, regulators, and contractors in India's civil aviation realm, increasing overall sector efficiencies. The TA ultimately developed a prototype system for one medium-sized airport in India as a proof of concept for future customization, deployment, and application of AGIS technologies for other airports in the country.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Safety Phase II Technical Assistance, through Wicks Group, PLLC
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION SAFETY PHASE II TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH WICKS GROUP, PLLC
long_description: This technical assistance (TA) grant is partially funding an Aviation Safety Project (ASP) Phase II for India's Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). The TA is assisting the DGCA in implementing additional improvements to its procedures, requirements and guidance, continuing to develop an appropriately-sized qualified inspectorate staff, completing further air carrier certifications, accomplishing aviation training organization certifications, and providing the DGCA with more on-the-job training regarding certification processes and requirements, specialized surveillance approaches and activities, and new oversight activities. The TA is also assisting in the recommendation of certification and licensing procedural changes that would better support business and general aviation growth in India, while also enabling appropriate oversight and monitoring of this aviation sub-sector..

title: Desk Study - Aviation Series 2017 Desk Study, through Montgomery Consulting Group, Inc.
short_description: DESK STUDY - AVIATION SERIES 2017 DESK STUDY, THROUGH MONTGOMERY CONSULTING GROUP, INC.
long_description: This desk study (DS) series has reviewed several potential and one ongoing East Asia aviation projects. The DS reviewed a preliminary concept proposal for two technical assistance projects on aviation meteorological forecasting and Massive Delay Response System (MDRS) under the U.S.- China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP), analyzed and made recommendations to modify the fourth phase of the China: General Aviation and Business Aviation Development Project, and reviewed and developed the Terms of Reference for training for HNA Aviation under a de minimis Training Grant.

long_description: This desk study (DS) series is reviewing and providing recommendations for up to three potential aviation activities in support of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP).

short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION SECURITY EQUIPMENT TESTING AND EVALUATION PROGRAM TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH HI-TEC SYSTEMS, INC.
long_description: This grant partially funded technical assistance (TA) to the Airports Authority of India (AAI) and India's Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) to establish an Aviation Security Equipment Testing and Evaluation Program (ASETEP). The ASETEP initiative included the development of clear standards, specifications, and testing procedures that align with international best practices for three types of aviation security equipment that scan carry-on luggage, checked baggage, and passengers.

short_description: ADMINISTRATION AND OVERSIGHT (INFRASTRUCTURE) - ADMINISTRATION AND OVERSIGHT (INFRASTRUCTURE)
long_description: Administration and Oversight (Infrastructure) - Supports the following illustrative program-funded costs: salaries of US, FSN, and TCN and other staff such as PSCs, RSSAs, PASAs, CASUs working for the US Government managing, administering, and supporting programs and their program-funded benefits such as housing, travel, transportation, education allowances etc, institutional contractors that provide such staff, rent, IT services, the program-funded share of utilities, staff training costs and the cost of developing and administering training programs, equipment and supplies, ICASS, vehicle fuel and maintenance, maintenance contracts, janitorial services, operational unit web page development and maintenance, outreach such as publications and the cost of their preparation (including staff costs), and technical assistance to ensure USG compliance with regulations.

title: 2017 U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: 2017 U.S.-INDIA AVIATION PARTNERSHIP SUMMIT, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: The 2017 U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit in Mumbai, India will include a two-day symposium, one day of site visits with U.S. companies and/or airport facilities in the Mumbai area, and a meeting of the U.S. - India Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). The sixth biennial U.S.-India Aviation Summit will convene a high-level dialogue, featuring U.S. technical and regulatory expertise that can support India's rapidly growing aviation market while addressing key issues related to aviation safety and security, standards harmonization, air traffic management and control, procurement practices, and, airspace utilization throughout India. Other focal points may include improving airport and airline operations, expanding business and general aviation, implementing environmental best practices, and overall system capacity expansion.

title: Technical Symposium - 2015 U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: TECHNICAL SYMPOSIUM - 2015 U.S.-INDIA AVIATION PARTNERSHIP SUMMIT, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: The fifth U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit featured a two-day Summit in Bangalore, India from November 3-5, 2015. With leadership provided by the U.S.-India Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP), the Summit received high-level support and attendees from India's civil aviation agencies (Ministry of Civil Aviation, Directorate General of Civil Aviation, Airports Authority of India, and Bureau of Civil Aviation Security), key industry members in the aviation sector, and the U.S. government (Departments of State, Commerce, and Transportation, including the Federal Aviation Administration as well as the Department of Homeland Security's Transportation Security Administration).

title: REBUILD following Typhoon Yolanda
long_description: Super Typhoon Haiyan (known in the Philippines as Yolanda) made landfall on November 8, 2013, killing more than 6,000 people and affecting 16 million more. Most of the public infrastructure across the central Philippine islands was demolished. The storm destroyed nearly 90% of the homes in Tacloban, the largest city in the affected area with a pre-storm population of 220,000.

short_description: TRADE-RELATED TRAINING PROGRAM - AIRCRAFT RESCURE AND FIREFIGHTING TRAINING PROJECT, THROUGH OSHKOSH AIRPORT PRODUCTS, LLC
long_description: This training grant to the Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) supported the training of representatives from Philippine airports through advanced firefighter and emergency response training as well as driver and operations training. Oshkosh Airport Products, LLC provided the majority of the training on-site in the Philippines, and also provided one week of training to senior CAAP officials in Oshkosh, WI at their headquarters.

title: Advancing Philippine Competitiveness (COMPETE) Project
long_description: The Advancing Philippine Competitiveness (COMPETE) Project is a four-year, Php774.5 million ($18.9 million) project began in October 2012. COMPETE assists the Philippines to improve its competitiveness to attain higher levels of trade and investment. To this end, the Project will provide technical assistance to enhance the regime for infrastructure provision, improve productivity in key industries, and increase access to credit. COMPETE is a USAID/Philippines project under the Partnership for Growth (PFG), a White House initiative. The PFG represents a partnership between the Philippines and the United States to promote broad-based and inclusive growth. The U.S. Government is working with the Government of the Philippines, the private sector and civil society organizations to help put the Philippines on an accelerated growth trajectory that benefits the majority of its population.

long_description: This Aviation Safety, Security and Airport Infrastructure Reverse Trade Mission brought an executive-level delegation of up to eight Indian aviation officials to the United States to further strategic bilateral cooperation and connect U.S. industry to commercial opportunities in support of India's growing aviation market.

title: Technical Assistance - Sustainable Master Planning For The Kolkata And Lucknow International Airports Technical Assistance, through Enterprise - United States Unknown
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - SUSTAINABLE MASTER PLANNING FOR THE KOLKATA AND LUCKNOW INTERNATIONAL AIRPORTS TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH ENTERPRISE - UNITE
long_description: This technical assistance grant to the Airports Authority of India (AAI) will deliver a twenty-year master and capital improvement plans to sustainably expand the operational capacities of the Kolkata and Lucknow International Airports. Landrum & Brown will conduct the master planning, which will incorporate environmental sustainability best practices, to accommodate the anticipated demand at two of the fastest growing airports within AAI's network.

title: Technical Assistance - Technical, Management and Operational Development Training (TMODT) Phase II Program, through Hi-Tec Systems, Inc.

long_description: This grant partially funded a technical assistance grant to the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) for Phase II of the Technical Management and Operational Development Training (TMODT) Program. This technical assistance (TA) initiative sought to enhance DGCA's regulatory capacity through the implementation of training courses and industry workshops for DGCA officials. The objective of this TA was to help DGCA develop an internal capacity for training new and current staff by creating a comprehensive, cohesive and sustainable program that leverages the knowledge and experience of senior aviation officials.

title: MCC Roads Project
long_description: MCC Funding will be used to reconstruct and rehabilitate 220 km of the Samar road crossing the provinces of Samar and Eastern Samar, of which approximately 180 km will undergo reconstruction/major rehabilitation while 40 km will receive only minor rehabilitation, as well as the replacement or upgrading of associated structures, such as bridges and culverts, to eliminate flooding and improve road safety. The road begins at the junction of Highway-Buray Wright (km 827+200) in Samar, and traverses eastward along primarily mountainous terrain to Taft (km 890+000). From Taft, it continues southward, along the coastline of Eastern Samar, ending in the town of Guiuan (km 1047+300). The section of road from Wright to Taft is an important east-west corridor providing inter-provincial connection between Samar and Eastern Samar. The section of the road from Taft to Guiuan provides the only access to 13 coastal municipalities. The capital of Eastern Samar, Borongan, is located centrally on this section of the road.

title: MCC Roads Project
long_description: Road Maintenance Improvement Technical Assistance Activity

title: Philippine-American Fund Grant Facility
long_description: The five-year Philippine-American Fund Grant Facility promotes sustainable development by competitively awarding grants to local organizations to sponsor activities in five broad categories - economic growth, governance, counter-trafficking, education and biodiversity conservation. The economic growth grants fund enterprise development and jobs generation. The governance grants strengthen the work of civil society organizations in promoting public sector accountability and transparency. The counter-trafficking grants assist local partners in improving trafficking prevention and prosecution and victim protection. The education grants finance scalable and technology-based solutions for quality education in hard-to-reach areas. The biodiversity grants foster conservation and management of natural resources to improve environmental resilience.

title: Technical Assistance - Shanghai Area Airspace And Ground Optimization Technical Assistance, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - SHANGHAI AREA AIRSPACE AND GROUND OPTIMIZATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA is funding the Shanghai Area Airspace and Ground Optimization Technical Assistance (TA) Program to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC). The TA, developed in close cooperation with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) and member companies of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program, is assisting CAAC to improve airspace capacity at the local, regional, and national system level, as well as to integrate airport planning with air carrier operations and air traffic procedures.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program 2017 Federal Aviation Administration (Faa) Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM 2017 FEDERAL AVIATION ADMINISTRATION (FAA) TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTAT
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities supporting the thirteenth phase of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) in FY 2017, including the twelfth phase of the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) and the ninth phase of the Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET) programs. This training and technical assistance aims to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies to gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - New Technologies For Airports Reverse Trade Mission Series, through Koeppen, Elliot & Associates, Ltd.
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - NEW TECHNOLOGIES FOR AIRPORTS REVERSE TRADE MISSION SERIES, THROUGH KOEPPEN, ELLIOT & ASSOCIATES, LTD.
long_description: USTDA is supporting a series of three airport Reverse Trade Missions (RTM) for China, each of which will familiarize up to 20 delegates from the China Aviation Administration of China and other relevant airport authorities with U.S. airport practices, equipment, and technology. The topics for the RTMs focus on airport emergency preparedness and response, smart and efficient airports, and hub airport operations and planning.

title: Technical Assistance - Airport Winter Operations Planning Technical Assistance, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIRPORT WINTER OPERATIONS PLANNING TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: This technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) is supporting the development of winter operations planning at the Harbin International Airport in northeastern China. This TA is intended to identify equipment, facilities, procedures and training solutions to mitigate flight delays and airport closures during extreme winter events. The TA will introduce CAAC to life-cycle costing concepts related to the procurement of ice and snow removal equipment.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Technical Assistance Workshops Phase Ii Federal Aviation Administration Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE WORKSHOPS PHASE II FEDERAL AVIATION ADMINISTRATION TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPA
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for the USTDA-funded China Aviation Technical Assistance (ATA) Workshops Phase II. The ATA Workshops aim to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Technical Assistance - Flight Delay Management, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - FLIGHT DELAY MANAGEMENT, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA's technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) developed in close cooperation with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), is assisting CAAC's Air Traffic Management Bureau (ATMB) to develop a systematic approach to assess factors that influence operational efficiency and capacity at the local, regional, and national system levels, as well as to integrate airport planning with air carrier operations and air traffic procedures. The project is promoting continued high-level bilateral engagement in the aviation sector and providing U.S. firms with greater access to senior CAAC leadership with the utmost influence in procurement decisions.

title: Technical Assistance - Flight Delay Management, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - FLIGHT DELAY MANAGEMENT, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities under the Flight Delay Management Technical Assistance (TA) Program, which is a U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) activity. The training and technical assistance aim to build strong relationships between U.S. and Chinese aviation sectors and assist the Chinese aviation market to continue to expand.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program 2017 Faa Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM 2017 FAA TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities supporting the thirteenth phase of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) in FY 2017, including the twelfth phase of the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) and the ninth phase of the Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET) programs. This training and technical assistance aims to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies to gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase Xi Federal Aviation Administration (Faa) Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM PHASE XI FEDERAL AVIATION ADMINISTRATION (FAA) TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPO
long_description: USTDA funding enabled the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities supporting the eleventh phase of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) in Fiscal Year 2015, including the tenth phase of the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) Program and the seventh Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET). This training and technical assistance aimed to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies to gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Technical Symposium - 2016 U.S.-China Aviation Symposium, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: TECHNICAL SYMPOSIUM - 2016 U.S.-CHINA AVIATION SYMPOSIUM, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: USTDA funding supported the 2016 U.S.-China Aviation Symposium, a bi-annual technical symposium (TS) held in the United States, followed by site visits. This symposium was intended to build upon prior symposiums and U.S.-China aviation events, and provide an excellent opportunity for senior U.S. and Chinese government officials and private sector aviation leaders to engage in substantive discussions on priority topics such as aviation sustainability, general aviation, airports and security, weather, managing the supply chain, new aviation technologies, and air traffic management, safety, efficiency, and capacity.

title: Desk Study - Helicopter Flight Skill and Maintenance Training, through Helicopter Support, Inc.
short_description: DESK STUDY - HELICOPTER FLIGHT SKILL AND MAINTENANCE TRAINING, THROUGH HELICOPTER SUPPORT, INC.
long_description: This training grant supported the Sikorsky Aircraft Corporation in its bid to supply 10 medium-weight helicopters to China's Zhuhai Helicopter Company. The grant provided funding for advanced helicopter maintenance and flight skill training for Zhuhai pilots and ground crew specializing in offshore activities.

title: Technical Assistance - Civil Aviation Sustainability Green Routes Initiative Technical Assistance, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - CIVIL AVIATION SUSTAINABILITY GREEN ROUTES INITIATIVE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA is funding in technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) for the U.S.-China Aviation Sustainability Green Routes Initiative program in support of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This TA is designed to promote the implementation of sustainable practices in the aviation sector and assess the feasibility of expanding the concept from one single test pilot flight to multiple flights and city pairs. The TA is assisting China in reducing its aviation-related emissions and providing opportunities for U.S. firms offering environmental technology and services.

title: Feasibility Study - Green Port Communications Pilot Project, through ARES Security Corporation
short_description: FEASIBILITY STUDY - GREEN PORT COMMUNICATIONS PILOT PROJECT, THROUGH ARES SECURITY CORPORATION
long_description: This grant is partially funding a pilot project and feasibility study to assist the China Waterborne Transport Research Institute with assessing the ability of U.S. technology to reduce the environmental impact of port operations at the Port of Qinghuangdao. The pilot project and feasibility study are demonstrating the capabilities of software developed by ARES Security Corporation and environmental monitoring systems developed by its partner, Triad Engineering Applications.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Freight Rail Operations Reverse Trade Missions, through Koeppen, Elliot & Associates, Ltd.
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - FREIGHT RAIL OPERATIONS REVERSE TRADE MISSIONS, THROUGH KOEPPEN, ELLIOT & ASSOCIATES, LTD.
long_description: This funding supported two rail sector reverse trade missions (RTM) for delegates from China's National Railways Administration (NRA) and various state-owned railway development companies in China. Each RTM was designed to familiarize up to 14 delegates with U.S. freight rail system practices, equipment, and technology. The first RTM under this contract took place and was completed in September 2016. The second RTM took place in September 2017.

title: Technical Assistance - Shanghai Area Airspace And Ground Optimization Technical Assistance Program Federal Aviation Administration (Faa) Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - SHANGHAI AREA AIRSPACE AND GROUND OPTIMIZATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM FEDERAL AVIATION ADMINISTRATION (FAA) TRANSFER FUND
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities under the Shanghai Area Airspace and Ground Optimization Technical Assistance Program, which is the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program activity. The TA is assisting the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) to improve airspace capacity at the local, regional, and national system level, as well as to integrate airport planning with air carrier operations and air traffic procedures.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program 2017, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM 2017, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA funding continued support for the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) with a thirteenth phase. This strategic, multi-program public-private partnership, launched by USTDA in 2004, has produced significant developmental results. Continuation of the program ensured an ongoing high-level dialogue with the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC), which continues to promote the interests of the U.S. aviation sector in China. ACP 2017 consisted of two complementary programs: the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) Phase VIII and Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET) Phase XI programs.

title: Civil Aviation Sustainability Green Routes Initiative Federal Aviation Administration Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: CIVIL AVIATION SUSTAINABILITY GREEN ROUTES INITIATIVE FEDERAL AVIATION ADMINISTRATION TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSP
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for the USTDA-funded U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Civil Aviation Sustainability -Green Routes- Initiative Technical Assistance (TA). The TA is envisioned to be the first of three phases under the Green Routes Pilot Program (GRPP), which aims to continue U.S.-China joint aviation-related energy conservation and emission reduction collaboration. The GRPP is intended to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Indonesia Air Traffic Management And Airport Infrastructure And Security Reverse Trade Mission, through Futron Corporation
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - INDONESIA AIR TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT AND AIRPORT INFRASTRUCTURE AND SECURITY REVERSE TRADE MISSION, THROUGH FUTRON CORPORATION
long_description: This Air Traffic Management and Airport Infrastructure and Security Reverse Trade Mission (RTM) brought delegates from the Indonesian civil aviation sector to the United States to gain exposure to advanced air traffic management and airport infrastructure and security technology, services and best practices through visits to U.S. facilities and meetings with leading U.S. companies. This RTM was held in February 2017.

title: Technical Assistance - Eastern Airspace Aviation Safety Technical Assistance, through Boeing Company
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - EASTERN AIRSPACE AVIATION SAFETY TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH BOEING COMPANY
long_description: This Eastern Airspace Aviation Safety technical assistance grant is funding the development of a Concept of Operations (ConOps) for the Directorate General of Civil Aviation of Indonesia (DGCA) to help improve the safety of flight and quality of air navigation in Papua and Maluku in the eastern region of Indonesia. The intended outcome of the ConOps is to provide a recommended implementation guideline for the Papua and Maluku airspace and aviation safety program goals and objectives, required capabilities, and the changes needed to realize the safety objectives. Over the long term the ConOps is designed to help increase aviation sector efficiencies, diminish operational inconsistencies in systems, reduce cost, and increase safety of the sector.

title: Technical Assistance - Air Simulation For The New Istanbul Airport Technical Assistance, through Jeppesen Sanderson, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIR SIMULATION FOR THE NEW ISTANBUL AIRPORT TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH JEPPESEN SANDERSON, INC.
long_description: This technical assistance grant is partially funding and supporting the design of the optimal airspace, runway approaches and air traffic control operating procedures for the New Istanbul Airport. The New Istanbul Airport is scheduled to open in 2018 and when completed in 2024, it will be one of the largest in the world, hosting 150 million passengers annually on flights to more than 350 destinations. Accordingly, ?GA Havaliman? I?letmesi A.?. (IGA), the concessionaire that will operate the airport for 25-years, is seeking technical assistance to ensure that the airspace operates at sufficient capacity to meet the anticipated traffic demand on the opening day in 2018.

title: Technical Assistance - Air Traffic Flow Management Implementation Plan Technical Assistance, through Enterprise - United States Unknown
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIR TRAFFIC FLOW MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION PLAN TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, THROUGH ENTERPRISE - UNITED STATES UNKNOWN
long_description: This technical assistance grant partially funds the development of an Air Traffic Flow Management master plan, which will provide a roadmap for deploying advanced U.S. Air Traffic Flow Management tools in Vietnam. The project is expected to increase air traffic efficiency and maintain strong safety practices in Vietnam while facilitating Vietnam's participation in regional Air Traffic Flow Management initiatives.

title: REBUILD following Typhoon Yolanda
long_description: Super Typhoon Haiyan (known in the Philippines as Yolanda) made landfall on November 8, 2013, killing more than 6,000 people and affecting 16 million more. Most of the public infrastructure across the central Philippine islands was demolished. The storm destroyed nearly 90% of the homes in Tacloban, the largest city in the affected area with a pre-storm population of 220,000.

title: Kalat - Quetta - Chaman Road Project (Section 2 & 4)
long_description: National Highway Authority (NHA), Ministry of Communication and Works, Government of Pakistan (GoP) initially started widening and improvement of KQC road (N-25) in 2006 with ADB Loan No. 2019. For execution purpose, the road was divided into four sections namely, (1) Kalat - Khad Kocha section, (2) Khad Kocha - Quetta section, (3) Quetta - Jungle Piralizai section, and (4) Jungle Piralizai - Chaman section. Sections 1 and 3 (covering 120 km) were completed in 2010. The remaining sections (2 and 4) covering 111 km could only partially be completed due to worst law and order situation and subsequent expiry of ADB loan. With an assurance of funding of US$ 90 million by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) in October, 2013, NHA assigned the balance / leftover works in sections 2 and 4 to M/s Frontier Works Organization (FWO) on EPC Lump sum basis in March 2014 for which formal contract agreement between NHA and FWO was signed on June 02, 2014.

title: Improvements to the rehabilitated Kalat-Quetta-Chaman Highway
short_description: IMPROVEMENTS TO THE REHABILITATED KALAT-QUETTA-CHAMAN HIGHWAY
long_description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) provided funding to for further improvements of the rehabilitated Kalat-Quetta-Chaman Highway, including a bypass road at Kuchlak, four bridges, two weighing stations, and three toll plazas.

title: Construction Monitoring and Evaluation Program (CMEP)
long_description: The Province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa suffered heavy infrastructure damages firstly due to terrorism and militancy and thereafter due to the unprecedented floods of July 2010. United States Agency for International Development (USAID) actively supported the rehabilitation and reconstruction activities of damaged schools, health facilities, drinking water supply schemes and other infrastructure components in Malakand Division by initially obligating funds amounting to USD 36 million under an Activity Agreement (No. 391-011) signed in September, 2009 between USAID and PaRRSA on behalf of GoP. Expanding its portfolio, USAID provided funds for rehabilitation / reconstruction of other infrastructure components including irrigation, bridges and roads etc. in the calamity hit areas. In June 2012, USAID sub-obligated additional funds for reconstruction of rains / floods damaged and bomb blasted schools in the affected districts of KP under Amended Activity Agreement No. 391-014. M/ AGES Consultants was assigned the task of Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) services of these reconstruction activities under USAID/Pakistan Construction Monitoring and Evaluation Program (CMEP), for which a formal contract was signed on September 30, 2012.

title: FATA Infrastructure Project (FIP)
long_description: The FATA Infrastructure Project (FIP) constructs roads to connect remote communities to markets and facilitate access to essential services, restores electricity infrastructure, and improves access to water for drinking and farming. This program is also supporting the construction of the Kalat-Quetta-Chaman road in Balochistan, an important cross-border trade route between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

title: Development Purchase Order For Storage Of Household Effects, through UniGroup, Inc.
short_description: DEVELOPMENT PURCHASE ORDER FOR STORAGE OF HOUSEHOLD EFFECTS, THROUGH UNIGROUP, INC.
long_description: This obligation funded storage expenses for FY 2017 associated with USTDA's personal services contract with Mr. Mark Dunn, Asia Regional Business Development Manager.

title: Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Air Traffic Management And Air Traffic Control Workshop, through The Webster Group
short_description: ASIA-PACIFIC ECONOMIC COOPERATION AIR TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT AND AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL WORKSHOP, THROUGH THE WEBSTER GROUP
long_description: USTDA funded a one-day regional Air Traffic Management (ATM) and Air Traffic Control (ATC) technical workshop in the Philippines. The activity was designed to disseminate the findings associated with a related activity, the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) Air Traffic Management Emissions Reduction Technical Assistance (TA). The workshop included: a) an overview session on ATM, Collaborative Decision Making (CDM), and other ATM/ATC concepts and the corresponding benefits of these technologies, and b) a detailed overview of the TA that the contractor performed to disseminate information to the entire APEC membership regarding the benefits of ATM and CDM tools and potential implementation methods, using the airspace in and between Vietnam and the Philippines as an example.

title: Desk Study - Aviation Sector Desk Study Series, through Montgomery Consulting Group, Inc.
short_description: DESK STUDY - AVIATION SECTOR DESK STUDY SERIES, THROUGH MONTGOMERY CONSULTING GROUP, INC.
long_description: This Desk Study (DS) series is reviewing, evaluating, and developing funding recommendations for up to four aviation sector feasibility studies, technical assistance or pilot projects in Southeast Asia for USTDA funding consideration. The aviation projects evaluated and prepared by the DS will support U.S. companies' entrance and/or expansion in various Southeast Asian markets while simultaneously assisting the countries in improving their aviation sectors.

title: FAA-Republic of the Marshall Islands
long_description: Agreement to send one (1) specialist with a background in airport safety and emergency planning to observe and validate full scale airport emergency exercises.

title: Desk Study - U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase Xii Desk Study, through Montgomery Consulting Group, Inc.
short_description: DESK STUDY - U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM PHASE XII DESK STUDY, THROUGH MONTGOMERY CONSULTING GROUP, INC.
long_description: This Desk Study reviewed a proposal for a thirteenth phase (Phase XIII) of technical assistance under the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). The ACP requested USTDA support for strategic assistance to provide training to China's civil aviation personnel and leaders in priority areas, including air traffic flow management and executive management development. This funding in the amount of $1,100 was added to the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase XIII desk study.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA continues its support for the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) with a twelfth phase. This strategic, multi-program, public-private partnership that USTDA launched in 2004 has produced significant developmental and U.S. export results. Continuation of the program ensured an ongoing high-level dialogue with the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC), which was intended to promote the interests of the U.S. aviation sector in China. ACP 2016 includes two components from previous phases that have been highly successful - the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) and Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET) programs.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Symposium 2018, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION SYMPOSIUM 2018, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: USTDA funding is supporting the 2018 U.S.-China Aviation Symposium, a bi-annual technical symposium (TS) to be held over a period of three days in Beijing, China in summer 2018. This symposium will build upon prior symposiums and U.S.-China aviation events, and provide an excellent opportunity for approximately 275 participants, including senior U.S. and Chinese government officials and private sector aviation leaders to engage in substantive discussions on priority topics such as aviation sustainability, general aviation, airports and security, weather, managing the supply chain, new aviation technologies, and air traffic management, safety, efficiency, and capacity.
